steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a complete subject — "Raymond Chang's textbook General Chemistry: The Essential Concepts" — but the blank must supply the main verb. Without a finite verb, the sentence is a fragment.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "discussing": A present participle cannot serve as a main verb on its own. The sentence would be a fragment. ✗
  • "discusses": A finite, present-tense, third-person singular verb that agrees with the singular subject and completes the sentence. ✓
  • "to discuss": An infinitive is non-finite and cannot act as the main verb of a clause. The sentence would be a fragment. ✗
  • "having discussed": A perfect participle is non-finite and cannot serve as a main verb. The sentence would be a fragment. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
